a_image.alt: Content-First Design style mood board — Design Philosophy · Content First

sections.a_eyebrow: Minimal · Content Heavy · Whitespace · Clean UI · Professional
sections.a_description: Typography as interface, whitespace as design, and the radical prioritization of readability over decoration — Content-First Design makes the content itself the primary visual element. Serif typography, generous line-height, and warm reading surfaces create an experience optimized for one thing: making words as comfortable and engaging to read on screen as they are on printed paper.

This reading-focused style powers long-form editorial platforms, blog engines, documentation sites, and literary publications. You'll see it on Medium, Substack, law firm publications, academic journals, and any platform where the words matter more than the wrapper — where the highest compliment is that the reader forgot the interface existed entirely.

a_image_cover.alt: Content-First Design style use case illustration
Content-First Design style use case illustration
Content-First Design style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The reading-optimized, content-driven aesthetic of content-first design — where typography leads, chrome disappears, and every design decision serves readability. Background: warm off-white (#fefefe). The .inner panel has an even warmer background (#faf9f6) with zero border and 8px border-radius — a reading surface, not a container. Colors: muted warm brown (#92400e) as the primary accent — the color of ink, of underlines, of editorial emphasis. Near-black (#1a1a1a) for headings, warm dark gray (#374151) for body text — optimized for long-form reading. The aesthetic is editorial and text-focused — the design IS the content, every pixel of chrome justified by its service to readability. Set in Merriweather — a screen-optimized serif designed specifically for comfortable on-screen reading at body sizes. Heading at weight 700, 26px, line-height 1.3, color #1a1a1a. Eyebrow at weight 300, 12px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #92400e. Body at weight 400, 16px, line-height 1.8, color #374151. Buttons: primary — warm brown (#92400e), white text, 6px border-radius. Secondary: transparent, brown border. Alternatives: Lora (elegant reading serif), Charter (screen-optimized serif).
